title: fraxinus viridis var. berlandieriana
intro: Fraxinus viridis var. berlandieriana, also known as Berlandier's green ash, is a variety of the green ash tree native to the south-central United States and northeastern Mexico. The medicinal properties of Berlandier's green ash include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of using Berlandier's green ash include its potential to treat fever, sore throats, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to support immune function and reduce inflammation. Berlandier's green ash can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves, bark, or roots in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
